must_detect.matched_text:
Lumenor Renewables SE – EU Data Processing and MNPI Handling Workflow for investors, employees, and counterparties on the Helios Regulated Market.
We process personal data under GDPR Art 6(1)(c) to comply with EU MAR and issuer obligations, Art 6(1)(f) for investor relations, and where Art 9 data arise (e.g., diversity indicators for CSRD), Art 9(2)(b) and (g) or explicit consent will be applied.
Categories include identification and contact details, PDMR dealing records, insider lists, and sustainability metrics prepared for CSRD/SFDR, minimised and pseudonymised where feasible.
Inside information is managed per EU MAR Article 17; we prohibit selective disclosure and make ad hoc announcements without delay via the Officially Appointed Mechanism (OAM) of the Helios Regulated Market.
We may delay disclosure under Article 17(4) where conditions are met and confidentiality is ensured, with subsequent notification to the competent authority and prompt publication via the OAM.
Closed periods under Article 19 apply 30 calendar days before interim and annual reports; PDMRs and closely associated persons are blocked from dealing during these blackout windows.
Issuer‑relative materiality is assessed using internal thresholds (e.g., ≥5% impact on EBITDA or covenant ratios), including sustainability-linked bond triggers, and draft CSRD KPIs may constitute MNPI if precise and price‑sensitive.
By contrast, our Q1 Trading Update filed on 15 May 2026 via the OAM and accompanied by a broad press release is public, and mere summaries of that filing are not MNPI.
Operational notes unrelated to the issuer’s financial instruments (such as refurbishing a staff canteen) are not regarded as inside information.
Personal data and MNPI may be processed by processors including OrbisCloud Ltd (Marindora) and Quayside Analytics GmbH (Vesland) under SCCs, following a transfer impact assessment and implementation of encryption, access controls, and event‑driven key rotation.
Special‑category data are not transferred outside the EEA unless a derogation applies or we deploy additional safeguards; any cross‑border transfer logs are retained for audit.
Insider lists are retained at least five years after the last update, deal‑registers for seven years, and CSRD working papers for three years, unless longer retention is mandated by applicable law.
Data subjects may exercise access, rectification, objection, restriction, and portability rights; submit requests to Ms. Sára Jelinek, Data Protection Officer, at dpo@lumenor-renewables.eu, and we will respond within one month.
Complaints may be lodged with the Coastal Union Data Authority, but we invite you to contact us first so we can address any concerns efficiently.
We train staff on market soundings, maintain project‑specific insider lists, and segregate deal rooms; only price‑sensitive precise information enters MAR workflows, while general ESG marketing materials are outside
